title: orobanche minor subsp. bovei
intro: Orobanche minor subsp. bovei, also known as Lesser Broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plants, typically those in the genus Thymus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Orobanche minor subsp. bovei include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
